levels.name: delightful climb
levels.description: The hardest platformer level in Geometry Dash, rated well over a year after its verification. The level consists of 5 distinct layers, fully deathless. The blocks crumble once they are stood on, requiring the player to go fast, all while maneuvering through unprecedentedly tight gaps as a mini robot.

levels.name: Diamonds For Dashers
levels.description: A former hardest rated platformer level, split up into two main sections, being only cube and only robot respectively. The two sections combined involve a wide variety of skillsets and must be done consecutively to clear the level. Also features custom voice lines played throughout the gameplay and break section.

levels.name: fake sunrise
levels.description: A simple robot needle level nerfed from a top 1 state, which involves collecting coins that remove spikes blocking the player's path, and beating a timer which kills the player if they are not on the final block when the vocals end. A majority of the difficulty is within the first third of the level. The ending contains a cutscene almost as long as the gameplay itself, though it can be skipped by clicking 4 times.

levels.name: i loved you
levels.description: The current hardest jetpack level, which divides its gameplay into multiple autoscrolling sections often requiring the player to keep up with the fast moving screen, all while needling through extremely tight gaps.

levels.name: CONVOLUTION
levels.description: A former top 1, considered far more difficult than the prior top 1 it had knocked off, Null. The level uniquely does not require the abuse of spike hitboxes, but instead relies on precise robot dropdowns and, most prominently, turnarounds. Most of the difficulty is in the beginning, but the "Don't Choke" segment in the final quarter of the level is also relatively hard.

levels.name: dish rags
levels.description: One of the hardest platformer autoscrollers in the game, void of any robot gameplay. It requires the player to go fairly fast and activate switches that slow the player down, all while performing precise jumps and orb clicks. The ending's decoration is in the style of the album in which the song is part of.

levels.name: glungus
levels.description: The hardest epic rated platformer level, containing incomprehensible high cps gameplay for 45 seconds. Despite the difficulty, the decoration style resembles a non demon. It also references the platformer community with it's many emojis. This level depicts a story with cutscenes that last longer than the gameplay itself. One of the most well recieved platformer levels, and described by some to be "a test of patience".

levels.name: IWannaPlatformerList
levels.description: One of the biggest megacollabs in platformer mode, made to celebrate the first year of The Platformer List. You are kidnapped and sent to TPL HQ, where you have to complete over 110+ floors to escape. Each floor is based on a different existing level from TPL, where it is created by the original creator of said level. There are over 130 different levels represented, all coming from TPL. The level drastically changes in difficulty, and can take over 24+ hours to complete in a full run, even with thorough practice.

levels.name: Rabbit Hole
levels.description: The second hardest of Yuko's 4 autoscrollers, which combines fast-paced gameplay and needle, making it especially difficult to properly line up jumps. The level was near-impossible in its original state, but ended up at top 3 when it was verified. The decoration is seemingly inspired by the song's music video.

levels.name: Crime and Punishment
levels.description: A fast-paced autoscroller level notable for its abstract decoration and gameplay. Many differing uses of shaders and particles can be seen throughout the level, and has a custom-built wave gamemode at 82%. This level received 3rd place at the GD 2025 Awards for "Best Extreme Platformer", and is currently the most enjoyed platformer extreme on the EDEL.

levels.name: SWAP CORE
levels.description: Very brutal level with fast paced blinking blocks and a massive difficulty spike at the 10th checkpoint.

levels.name: cabbit
levels.description: Named after the emoji of the same name, this level is a recreation of a Celeste grandmaster map. It has recreated mechanics from the game like the feather, kevin blocks, touch switches, and even the crystal heart at the end. It also spikes up in difficulty for the last two checkpoints, that's not very peaceful...

levels.name: HYPER GRAVITRON
levels.description: Minigame level based off of a section in the game VVVVVV by Terry Cavanagh. Has randomized obstacles to avoid while constantly flipping gravity. Survive for two minutes to beat the level, and earn coins for surviving even longer.

levels.name: ENTANGLEMENT (Solo)
levels.description: 2-player minigame level where you play two instances of Snake on the same screen. The controls are left/right relative to the direction the snake is moving in, instead of moving in the input direction. Collect 25 'apples' to win.

levels.name: Hexagonestestestest
levels.description: Minigame level based off of the game Super Hexagon by Terry Cavanagh. Avoid the randomized obstacles for 8 minutes. Has a checkpoint at every minute mark.

levels.name: LIMU
levels.description: The only level in the -imu series without fixed hitboxes, and instead features weird needle jumps. This makes the level poorly recieved, and seen as the worst -imu level.

levels.name: Pimu
levels.description: The first level in the -imu series. Features the two main mechanics in the series, that being low gravity and fixed hitboxes. The level was named after Santa Catalina island.

levels.name: rimu
levels.description: One of the levels in the imu series, with the r standing for rage. Foddian map with very little safe zones, making it play like your typical deathless level. Surprisingly laggy for only being made in one week. When reaching the end of the level, you eat the moon. yummy!

levels.name: Timu
levels.description: One of the better levels  in the -imu series decoration wise, with its dark factory atmosphere. Uses square hitboxes instead of the usual circles.

levels.name: Robot King
levels.description: An early 2.2 pixel-style level inspired by Jump King, where you must climb 5 zones as the robot to reach the top. It’s designed to be rage-inducing, being structured in a way that one missed jump can make you fall down many screens, while also incorporating ice physics and slopes. The last 2 zones are especially hard.

levels.name: Cimu
levels.description: One of the levels in the -imu series. This level has 3 sections that have different skillsets ranging from speedrunning, blink block timings, and slopes.

levels.name: Qimu
levels.description: The third level in the -imu series. Unlike the original, Qimu does have checkpoints. The first checkpoint is a robot part with typical -imu jumps, the second is a ball with weird alignment clicks, and the third is a very easy cube.

levels.name: DETERNARY
levels.description: One of the most unique extremes. This level contains a ternary computation machine in which you must input code to perform five specific tasks.  Requires a high level of coding skill to complete.

levels.name: Walk to the midnight
levels.description: A "Dead Of Night" remake made by only 1 person, featuring easier gameplay and different styled rooms
